# Wrangling {.unnumbered} ![](images/wrangling.png){width="650" fig-align="center"}

**Data wrangling transforms raw data into a structured format.**

The process includes collecting, tidying, and preprocessing raw data so that it can be easily analyzed and visualised. We will use ```pandas``` for these tasks. ## Reading ::: {.callout-tip} ### What types of data files do you generate in your research? ::: First, we need to access our data and store it in a variable. Pandas has a function that allows us to *read in* an excel document: ```read_excel()```. ::: {.cell output-location='fragment' execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} # import pandas, aliased as pd import pandas as pd # Import an Excel file called "example" from the data folder df = pd.read_excel('data/example_proteins.xlsx', sheet_name='Sheet1') # Visualise the first few rows print(df.head(2)) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Protein Molecular Weight (kDa) Function \ 0 HBB_Hemoglobin 64.5 Oxygen transport 1 ACTA1_Actin 42.0 Cell structure Subcellular Location 0 Cytoplasm 1 Cytoplasm ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Visualise the first few rows df.head(2) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ```{=html}
Protein Molecular Weight (kDa) Function Subcellular Location
0 HBB_Hemoglobin 64.5 Oxygen transport Cytoplasm
1 ACTA1_Actin 42.0 Cell structure Cytoplasm
``` ::: ::: Pandas has many other ```read``` functions for all types of data. ![](images/pandas_read.png){width="700" fig-align="center"} ::: {.cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} # From a plain text document: df = pd.read_table('data/example_proteins.txt', delimiter='\t') # From a csv document: df = pd.read_csv('data/example_proteins.csv') ``` ::: ## Cleaning columns Cleaning standardises and simplifies a dataset. This might involve adjusting column names, correcting data types, and separating pieces of information from one column into many. We can access the column names for a dataframe using ```.columns```. ::: {.cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} print(df.columns) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Index(['Protein', 'Molecular Weight (kDa)', 'Function', 'Subcellular Location'], dtype='object') ``` ::: ::: We can drop columns by name using the ```.drop()``` method. ::: {.cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} df.drop('Subcellular Location', axis=1) df.head(2) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=6} ```{=html}
Protein Molecular Weight (kDa) Function Subcellular Location
0 HBB_Hemoglobin 64.5 Oxygen transport Cytoplasm
1 ACTA1_Actin 42.0 Cell structure Cytoplasm
``` ::: ::: We can access the column names for a dataframe using ```.columns```. ::: {.cell execution_count=8} ``` {.python .cell-code} print(df.columns) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Index(['Protein', 'Molecular Weight (kDa)', 'Function', 'Subcellular Location'], dtype='object') ``` ::: ::: We can drop columns by name using the ```.drop()``` method. ::: {.cell execution_count=9} ``` {.python .cell-code} df.drop('Subcellular Location', axis=1, inplace=True) df.head(2) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=9} ```{=html}
Protein Molecular Weight (kDa) Function
0 HBB_Hemoglobin 64.5 Oxygen transport
1 ACTA1_Actin 42.0 Cell structure
``` ::: ::: ::: {.callout-note} ### Explore other methods available for DataFrame operations [here](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/reference/api/pandas.DataFrame.html#:~:text=of%20the%20DataFrame.-,Methods,-abs()). ::: We can update the column names for a dataframe by directly updating the ```.columns```, or using the ```.rename()``` method. ::: {.cell execution_count=10} ``` {.python .cell-code} df.columns = ['protein', 'molecular_weight_kda', 'function'] df.head(2) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=10} ```{=html}
protein molecular_weight_kda function
0 HBB_Hemoglobin 64.5 Oxygen transport
1 ACTA1_Actin 42.0 Cell structure
``` ::: ::: We can update the column names for a dataframe by directly updating the ```.columns```, or using the ```.rename()``` method. ::: {.cell execution_count=11} ``` {.python .cell-code} df.rename(columns={ 'Protein': 'protein', 'Molecular Weight (kDa)': 'molecular_weight_kda', 'Function': 'function'}, inplace=True) df.head(2) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=11} ```{=html}
protein molecular_weight_kda function
0 HBB_Hemoglobin 64.5 Oxygen transport
1 ACTA1_Actin 42.0 Cell structure
``` ::: ::: ::: {.callout-note} ### Adopt variable naming conventions (all lowercase characters, separated by underscores) to make column names consistent and easy to access. ::: To select individual columns, we can then use ```[colname]``` or ```.colname```. ::: {.columns} ::: {.column} :::{.fragment} ::: {.cell execution_count=12} ``` {.python .cell-code} df['protein'] ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=12} ``` 0 HBB_Hemoglobin 1 ACTA1_Actin 2 COL1A1_Collagen 3 INS_Insulin 4 MYH7_Myosin 5 CYC1_Cytochrome c 6 KRT18_Keratin 7 ALB_Albumin 8 TUBA1B_Tubulin 9 FTH1_Ferritin 10 CAT_Catalase 11 GAPDH_GAPDH 12 FGA_Fibrinogen 13 ELN_Elastin 14 H1F0_Histone H1 15 LCT_Lactase 16 TP53_p53 17 CTRC_Chymotrypsin 18 HSPA1A_HSP70 19 AMY1A_Alpha-amylase 20 HK1_Hexokinase 21 COL2A1_Collagen type II 22 ACTB_Beta-actin 23 TTN_Troponin T 24 UBB_Ubiquitin 25 LAMA2_Laminin 26 ITGB1_Integrin 27 PRDX1_Peroxiredoxin 28 CASP3_Caspase-3 29 SOD1_SOD1 30 TF_Transferrin 31 GCG_Glucagon 32 F2_Thrombin 33 NEFH_Neurofilament 34 CALM1_Calmodulin 35 PYGM_Glycogen phosphorylase 36 HRAS_Ras protein 37 COL3A1_Collagen type III 38 COL4A1_Collagen type IV 39 PKLR_Pyruvate kinase 40 AK1_Adenylate kinase 41 COL5A1_Collagen type V 42 COL6A1_Collagen type VI 43 LAMB2_Laminin beta 2 44 ND1_NADH dehydrogenase 45 ATP5F1_ATP synthase Name: protein, dtype: object ``` ::: ::: ::: ::: ::: {.column} :::{.fragment} ::: {.cell execution_count=13} ``` {.python .cell-code} df.protein ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=13} ``` 0 HBB_Hemoglobin 1 ACTA1_Actin 2 COL1A1_Collagen 3 INS_Insulin 4 MYH7_Myosin 5 CYC1_Cytochrome c 6 KRT18_Keratin 7 ALB_Albumin 8 TUBA1B_Tubulin 9 FTH1_Ferritin 10 CAT_Catalase 11 GAPDH_GAPDH 12 FGA_Fibrinogen 13 ELN_Elastin 14 H1F0_Histone H1 15 LCT_Lactase 16 TP53_p53 17 CTRC_Chymotrypsin 18 HSPA1A_HSP70 19 AMY1A_Alpha-amylase 20 HK1_Hexokinase 21 COL2A1_Collagen type II 22 ACTB_Beta-actin 23 TTN_Troponin T 24 UBB_Ubiquitin 25 LAMA2_Laminin 26 ITGB1_Integrin 27 PRDX1_Peroxiredoxin 28 CASP3_Caspase-3 29 SOD1_SOD1 30 TF_Transferrin 31 GCG_Glucagon 32 F2_Thrombin 33 NEFH_Neurofilament 34 CALM1_Calmodulin 35 PYGM_Glycogen phosphorylase 36 HRAS_Ras protein 37 COL3A1_Collagen type III 38 COL4A1_Collagen type IV 39 PKLR_Pyruvate kinase 40 AK1_Adenylate kinase 41 COL5A1_Collagen type V 42 COL6A1_Collagen type VI 43 LAMB2_Laminin beta 2 44 ND1_NADH dehydrogenase 45 ATP5F1_ATP synthase Name: protein, dtype: object ``` ::: ::: ::: ::: ::: :::{.fragment} ::: {.callout-important} ### The ```.``` notation only works when the column names do not have spaces. ::: ::: ## Cleaning data types The ```dtype``` of each column dictates what operations we can do with it's data. ::: {.cell execution_count=15} ``` {.python .cell-code} print(df.dtypes) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` protein object molecular_weight_kda object function object subcellular_location object expression_log2 float64 time_h int64 donor_id object dtype: object ``` ::: ::: We can convert between data types using the ```.astype()``` method. ::: {.cell output-location='fragment' execution_count=16} ``` {.python .cell-code} df['molecular_weight_kda'].astype(float) print(df.dtypes) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` protein object molecular_weight_kda object function object subcellular_location object expression_log2 float64 time_h int64 donor_id object dtype: object ``` ::: ::: The ```dtype``` of each column dictates what operations we can do with it's data. ::: {.cell execution_count=17} ``` {.python .cell-code} print(df.dtypes) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` protein object molecular_weight_kda object function object subcellular_location object expression_log2 float64 time_h int64 donor_id object dtype: object ``` ::: ::: We can convert between data types using the ```.astype()``` method. ::: {.cell execution_count=18} ``` {.python .cell-code} df['molecular_weight_kda'] = df['molecular_weight_kda'].astype(float) print(df.dtypes) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` protein object molecular_weight_kda float64 function object subcellular_location object expression_log2 float64 time_h int64 donor_id object dtype: object ``` ::: ::: Often string data are composites joined by one or more *delimiters*. To use these data elements we need to separate elements into different columns using the ```.str``` and ```.split``` methods. ::: {.columns} ::: {.column} ::: {.cell execution_count=19} ``` {.python .cell-code} df['protein'] ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=19} ``` 0 HBB_Hemoglobin 1 ACTA1_Actin 2 COL1A1_Collagen 3 INS_Insulin 4 MYH7_Myosin ... 685 COL5A1_Collagen type V 686 COL6A1_Collagen type VI 687 LAMB2_Laminin beta 2 688 ND1_NADH dehydrogenase 689 ATP5F1_ATP synthase Name: protein, Length: 690, dtype: object ``` ::: ::: ::: ::: {.column} ::: {.cell execution_count=20} ``` {.python .cell-code} df['protein'].str.split('_') ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=20} ``` 0 [HBB, Hemoglobin] 1 [ACTA1, Actin] 2 [COL1A1, Collagen] 3 [INS, Insulin] 4 [MYH7, Myosin] ... 685 [COL5A1, Collagen type V] 686 [COL6A1, Collagen type VI] 687 [LAMB2, Laminin beta 2] 688 [ND1, NADH dehydrogenase] 689 [ATP5F1, ATP synthase] Name: protein, Length: 690, dtype: object ``` ::: ::: ::: ::: ## Cleaning composite data Often string data are composites joined by one or more *delimiters*. To use these data elements we need to separate elements into different columns using the ```.str``` and ```.split``` methods. ::: {.cell execution_count=21} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Assign split strings to separate columns using 'expand' keyword df[['protein_name', 'gene']] = df['protein'].str.split('_', expand=True) df.head(2) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=21} ```{=html}
protein molecular_weight_kda function subcellular_location expression_log2 time_h donor_id protein_name gene
0 HBB_Hemoglobin 64.5 Oxygen transport Cytoplasm 0.111781 2 A17362 HBB Hemoglobin
1 ACTA1_Actin 42.0 Cell structure Cytoplasm 0.203740 2 A17362 ACTA1 Actin
``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell execution_count=22} ``` {.python .cell-code} df.to_csv(f'data/clean_data.csv') ``` ::: ::: {.callout-note} ### These operations will be very dataset specific, and each dataset will likely require a combination of approaches. ::: ## In practice ::: {.callout-caution} ### Exercises 3.1 Reading and cleaning :::
